title: Ensure AWS Organizations changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure AWS Organizations changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes made to AWS Organizations in the master AWS account.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring AWS Organizations changes can help you prevent unwanted, accidental, or intentional modifications that may lead to unauthorized access or other security breaches. This monitoring technique helps ensure that any unexpected changes made within your AWS Organizations can be investigated and that any unwanted changes can be rolled back.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> § Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> § Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: 3. aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 4. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: 5. "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventSource = organizations.amazonaws.com) && (($.eventName = "AcceptHandshake") || ($.eventName = "AttachPolicy") || ($.eventName = "CreateAccount") || ($.eventName = "CreateOrganizationalUnit") || ($.eventName = "CreatePolicy") || ($.eventName = "DeclineHandshake") || ($.eventName = "DeleteOrganization") || ($.eventName = "DeleteOrganizationalUnit") || ($.eventName = "DeletePolicy") || ($.eventName = "DetachPolicy") || ($.eventName = "DisablePolicyType") || ($.eventName = "EnablePolicyType") || ($.eventName = "InviteAccountToOrganization") || ($.eventName = "LeaveOrganization") || ($.eventName = "MoveAccount") || ($.eventName = "RemoveAccountFromOrganization") || ($.eventName = "UpdatePolicy") || ($.eventName = "UpdateOrganizationalUnit")) }" 6. Note the <organizations-changes> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 7. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <organizations-changes> captured in step 4: 8. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<organizations-changes>]' 9. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 10. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: 11. aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for AWS Organizations changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: 2. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <organizations-changes> --metric-transformations metricName=<organizations-changes>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventSource = organizations.amazonaws.com) && (($.eventName = "AcceptHandshake") || ($.eventName = "AttachPolicy") || ($.eventName = "CreateAccount") || ($.eventName = "CreateOrganizationalUnit") || ($.eventName = "CreatePolicy") || ($.eventName = "DeclineHandshake") || ($.eventName = "DeleteOrganization") || ($.eventName = "DeleteOrganizationalUnit") || ($.eventName = "DeletePolicy") || ($.eventName = "DetachPolicy") || ($.eventName = "DisablePolicyType") || ($.eventName = "EnablePolicyType") || ($.eventName = "InviteAccountToOrganization") || ($.eventName = "LeaveOrganization") || ($.eventName = "MoveAccount") || ($.eventName = "RemoveAccountFromOrganization") || ($.eventName = "UpdatePolicy") || ($.eventName = "UpdateOrganizationalUnit")) }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 3. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: 4. aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 5. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: 6. aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 7. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: 8. aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <organizations-changes> --metric-name <organizations-changes> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.default_value: By default, CloudTrail logs AWS Organizations events (e.g., CreateAccount, AttachPolicy, DeleteOrganization), but no CloudWatch metric filters or alarms exist. These changes are recorded but not actively monitored unless configured.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
- The audit approach will be automated as the required properties can be queried using AWS APIs and validated through the AWS CLI commands provided in the audit section. 

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_metric_filter_monitoring_organizations_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **Identify Active Multi-region CloudTrail Trail:**
   - Use the command: `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
   - Look for trails with `"IsMultiRegionTrail": true`. Also, ensure the trail is active by using the command: `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>` and confirm that `"IsLogging"` is true.

2. **Event Selectors for Management Events:**
   - Use: `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
   - Validate for an event selector where:
     - `"IncludeManagementEvents"` is true
     - `"ReadWriteType"` is set to `All`.

3. **Retrieve CloudWatch Log Group Name:**
   - From the trail description, extract the value associated with `"CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn"` to obtain the Log Group Name.

4. **List Metric Filters:**
   - Use: `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>`
   - Check if there is a metric filter present using the filter pattern provided:
     ```
     { 
       ($.eventSource = organizations.amazonaws.com) && 
       (($.eventName = "AcceptHandshake") || 
        ($.eventName = "AttachPolicy") || 
        ...
        ($.eventName = "UpdateOrganizationalUnit"))
     }
     ```

5. **Check Alarms Associated with Metric Filters:**
   - Command: `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<organizations-changes>]'`
   - Ensure there is at least one active alarm associated with the metric filter that captures the changes.

6. **Check for Active SNS Subscription:**
   - Retrieve the `AlarmActions` from the previous command's output to get the SNS topic ARN.
   - Use: `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`.
   - Validate that there is at least one subscription with a valid `SubscriptionArn`.

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- **Pass**: 
  - All checks from steps 1 through 6 yield expected conditions (active multi-region trail, correct event selectors, metric filter exists, active alarms, and valid SNS subscriptions).
- **Fail**:
  - Any check does not meet the expected conditions.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The tasks outlined can be effectively performed using AWS APIs, boto3, or AWS CLI. AWS provides comprehensive APIs for CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS, which can be leveraged to automate the monitoring of AWS Organizations changes.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly accurate. The use of AWS CLI commands is appropriate for the tasks described. However, there are minor areas for improvement:
  - The command for checking alarms should ensure it accurately filters based on the metric name related to the organizations changes.
  - The program name could be more descriptive to reflect the specific security check being performed.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- **Program Name:** The current program name `aws_cloudtrail_metric_filter_monitoring_organizations_changes` is somewhat descriptive but could be improved for clarity. A suggestion could be `aws_cloudtrail_monitor_org_changes_metric_filter`.
- **Alternative Methods:** Consider using AWS Config to continuously monitor and evaluate the configuration of AWS resources, which could provide an additional layer of compliance checking.
- **Additional Checks:** Ensure that the CloudTrail logs are encrypted and that the SNS topic has appropriate access policies to prevent unauthorized access.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured, and the use of AWS CLI commands is appropriate for automating the checks. The approach aligns with AWS security best practices for monitoring changes in AWS Organizations.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- **Modify:** Update the program name for better clarity and ensure that the alarm check accurately filters based on the specific metric name related to AWS Organizations changes. Consider incorporating AWS Config for continuous compliance monitoring as an enhancement.
